description:
At first glance, the aesthetic appears balanced and minimal. But closer inspection reveals subtle divergences in texture, structure and finish. These differences are deliberate. The creative team at Hooker & Young explores the idea thattrue beauty does not lie in perfect replication, but in the tension between harmony and individuality. One silhouette may carry a slightly sharper edge, the other a softer transition. One texture may appear more polished, the other more lived-in.
The effect is powerful: symmetry becomes a narrative device rather than a technical exercise. As Gary Hooker and Michael Young explain, the concept was to “reflect strength through alignment, while allowing each model’s character to breathe through the detail.” This approach transforms each image into a dialogue between two personalities, unified yet distinct.

trend_name: Contemporary Hair Sculpting with Cool Minimalism
description:
Technically, ‘Echoes’ demonstrates refined control and architectural precision. The silhouettes are clean, sculptural and intentional. Lines are deliberate; weight distribution is carefully considered; negative space becomes as important as volume.
The collection embraces cool-toned minimalism — muted palettes, controlled shine, and an almost editorial restraint. This aesthetic choice allows the craftsmanship to take centre stage,reinforcing Hooker & Young’s reputation for elegance infused with modernity.
CreditsHair:Gary Hooker & Michael YoungPhotography:Michael YoungMake-up:Kirsten BaillieStylist:Hooker & YoungImages:FPA

trend_name: ‘Disrupt’: the defining collection behind Lucía Siero’s London victory
description:
“This collection came from a period of challenge and growth— pushing myself creatively and personally to keep going. Each cut reflects breaking patterns, letting go of control, and finding freedom in the unknown,” explainsLucía Siero.The project emerged from a time of both personal and professional challenge, wherediscomfort became a driving force for creativity. Every cut embodies the breaking of established patterns, the surrender of absolute control and the pursuit of freedom in uncharted territory.
From a technical perspective, ‘Disrupt’ representsa precise alchemy between structure and movement. Geometric foundations coexist with dynamic textures that bring fluidity and character to each look. It is an exercise in balance, where the discipline of cutting meets a more visceral and emotional approach. “Each look speaks about how, through consistency and courage, we evolve,” Lucía adds.
With this collection,Lucía Siero was crowned London Hairdresser of the Year atthe latest edition of the British Hairdressing Awards,consolidating her international positioning and sending a clear message to the industry: true innovation is born when we dare to embrace discomfort and trust the creative process.
CreditsHair:Lucía Siero at Toni&GuyColour:Stuart MatuskaPhoto:Kevin LuchmunMake-up:Monnie KaurStyling:Giada Veronesi

trend_name: Illume Collection:  Anna Pacitto Presents a Visionary Blend of Natural Texture and Futuristic Edge
description:
The Illume Collection represents a profound exploration of light as a living current rather than a simple visual effect.Anna Pacittodrew inspiration from the raw textures of nature and the sharp, sleek edges found in futurism. Each look in this series channels an ethereal energy that feels both deeply organic and strikingly modern. This vision showcases hair with perfect sculpture, while maintaining a sense of movement and vitality. The collection serves as a testament to her mastery within the prestigious NAHA Master Category this year.
The artist wanted to explore the powerful contrast that exists between soft elements and very sharp, defined lines. This intentional juxtaposition allows matte finishes to meet metallic highlights for a truly dynamic and multi-dimensional result. Every strand of hair reflects a pulse and to live with its own unique glow. By blending these opposing forces, the collection achieves a balance that feels both grounded and incredibly futuristic. This approach highlights the evolution of technical skill through a lens of pure artistic instinct.
For Anna, achieving mastery in the professional industry is not about reaching perfection but about embracing constant evolution. The collection represents her personal growth as a visionary who continues to push the boundaries of hair design. Her technique is lit from within, reflecting a deep understanding of how hair moves and catches the light. This specific vision allows the hair to transcend traditional styling and become a form of living, breathing art. The result is a body of work that feels both technically superior and emotionally resonant.
The textures within the collection offer a fresh perspective on how to manipulate hair for high-fashion editorial work. Using both instinctive movements and disciplined techniques, Anna creates shapes that feel edgy yet soft at the same time. These looks serve as an inspiration for stylists who want to master the art of contrast in their work. By focusing on the energy of light, the collection provides a roadmap for creating hair that glows with purpose. Each silhouette is designed to stand out as a unique piece of a larger, cohesive story.
Hair:Anna Pacitto@anna_pacitto_pure Photo: @johnrawson assisted by @pablono1 Team: @instacolorjosee @cynthiavdt_ Vieilledent @zoeboysen1 MU: @ekat_ulya @nathan.archambo Styling: @florenceodurand @huguesbergevin Model: @montagemodels @catherine.dupont Acknowledgements: Salon Pure @salonpuremtl @davinesnorthamerica @davinescolor @benoit4955

trend_name: The Symbolic Collection – Redefining Strength Through Bold Crimson Tones And Scandinavian Street Culture
description:
TheSymbolic Collectionmarks a powerful creative statement from the 2025Fellowship for British Hairdressing’s FAME Team. Drawing inspiration from the emotional intelligence of the colour red, the series explores strength across different historical periods. The team utilised diverse cultural references to build a narrative that feels both ancient and incredibly modern. Sharp silhouettes influenced byScandinavian street cultureprovide a structured edge to the vibrant and artistic hair designs. This collection showcases the incredible technical range and visionary thinking of the newest generation of British hair talent. It serves as a celebration of how colour can communicate deep human emotions and cultural identities simultaneously.
The colour red was chosen as the primary focus because of its historic association with passion and vitality. Within TheSymbolic Collection, various shades of crimson and scarlet are used to define the overall creative mood. The FAME Team explored how this specific palette has represented different meanings across time and global cultures. By layering these tones, they created a sense of depth that draws the eye into the intricate textures. This focus on a single, dominant colour allowed the team to push their artistic boundaries with great precision. The result is a cohesive body of work that feels both visually striking and emotionally resonant.
Creative Direction: Christel Barron-Hough @christelbarronhough Photographer: Alex Barron-Hough @alexbarronhough Photographer Assistants: Ken Street Digital Technician: Gideon Marshall Stylist: Clare Frith @ clarefrithcreative Make-up artist: Katie Moore @katiemooremua Sponsor: Wella @wellaprofessionalsuki Hair: FAME Team 2025 Annie Franklin @anniefranklinhair Stevie Leah Loscombe @Stevieleahstylist Sam Bickle @sammybickle_hair Bekki Delehedy @bekkidhair

trend_name: OVERLAPS: Stefano Bugada’s Colour Redefining Collection
description:
Contemporary beauty thrives on influences, overlaps, and intertwining languages. This vision gives rise toOVERLAPS, the new hair fashion collection byStefano Bugada, a project that celebrates colour as a form of expression, as energy, as well as an identity. The title itself refers to the idea of ​​layering: of techniques, emotions, and nuances that intertwine to tell unique and unrepeatable stories.
With OVERLAPS, Bugada confirms a stylistic signature. it’s an approach that blends aesthetic research, technical experimentation, and also a chromatic language capable of transforming hair into a living canvas. Each look is conceived as a piece of a larger mosaic. Each is an exploration of colour as well as shape that reflects a contemporary and bold vision of hairstyling.
A woman with a strong personality, driven by the desire to detach herself from objective reality to live in a creative environment. Elegance translates into a sophisticated and contemporary undulation, combining strength and also feminine expression. The textures, enriched bychromatic overlays, generate a fascinating and enigmatic diffusion effect, dynamic and surprising.
It combines the elegance of luxury with a street-chic soul, in a deliberately messy look that plays with stylistic and color crossovers. The movement of the hair, inspired by the timeless grace ofBotticelli’s Venus, translates into a contemporary and dynamic aesthetic. Earthy tones create colour contrasts that express energy as well as harmony, giving rise to an ethereal and captivating beauty, imbued with mystery and imaginable stories.
A curly, voluminouslook , a symbol of strength and independence. Awoman with a rebellious streakwho faces and overcomes every adversity, transforming herself into a vital and essential element. The cut, styling, and colour blend into acontemporary and sophisticated style, where contrasting hues interact, creating a bold aesthetic. A look that reveals a strong, unconventional, and irredeemably fascinating attitude.
Geometric lines and horizontal graphic colours intertwine to createan artistic as well as futuristic design. This is capable of capturing the eye and redefining the visual identity. It’sa visionary and sophisticated look, designed for those who refuse to go unnoticed. Furthermore, linear and clean character is enriched withunusual yet elegant colour contrasts, which enhance a bold and unconventional femininity. An aesthetic that speaks to the strength of those who are always oriented towards change.
A look that expressescontrast, diversity, and provocation, becoming the symbol of a contemporary as well as universal language. The cut, full around the perimeter and graduated at the top, conveys arefined and contemporary style, enlivened by contrasting colours that powerfully interpret the identity of the woman who wears it.
ART DIR.STEFANO BUGADAPH.MAX VALERIOPRES & EDU DIR.TONY PROIETTICREATIVE DIR.EMILIANO FRATARCANGELIFASHION DIR.GIUSEPPE TESSIERMASTER EDU.ELEONORA TESSIER & LUCA LOMBARDOSTYLISTLIA CERVIMUAROBERTA SIANI

Cultural differences, traditions, and customs distinguish women around the world, but emotions make them profoundly similar. It is in beauty that all women recognize themselves; beyond cultural differences, traditions, and customs distinguish women around the world. This is why hair is and remains the most immediate means of expressing one’s identity.
WithFramesi Global Style, the gaze expands beyond borders: Italian taste dialogues with international sensibilities, finding a balance between roots and contemporary interpretations. The collections are born from the contribution of a global team of hairstylists, each with a cultural background of experience and technique, in a creative dialogue that enriches each proposal.
Within thePeople Collection, three distinct feminine expressions emerge: the sophisticated intensity ofEdgy, the romantic yet bold lightness ofFlirty, and the surprising essentiality ofMaxiMinimalist, where simplicity is illuminated by unexpected details.
Light and freedom
Soft volumes, enhanced with theatrical flashes of light that speak of freedom and self-confidence. A cut and colour that convey sensual lightness.
An extreme cut with strong colours and contrasts, which celebrate a mischievous innocence, a new romanticism made of alternating softness and sharp edges.

A deconstructed cut with an easy-to-wear style, balanced volumes, and opulent colour. A simplicity that surprises with elaborate details.
Full credits of the collection:
Project coordinator: Patrizia Caldera
Shooting coordinator: Container Communication
Hairstylists: Italian Style Framesi Energy Style Framesi Global Style Framesi
Make-up artist: Silvia Dell’Orto
Photographer: Karel Losenicky
Art director: Leila Palermo – Next Agency
Video director: Alessandro Dalla Fontana
Fashion consultants: Paolo Lungo
Copywriters: Ilaria Pizzoferrato and Patrizia Annovazzi
Press agency: Sintesi.Biz

trend_name: Dialekt Collection: A New Visual Language for Hair from TONI&GUY
description:
TONI&GUYintroduces theDialekt Collectionas their 2026 creative cut and colour launch. The Dialekt Collection explores hair as our language while embracing regional cultures and the unique fabric of individual hair. This global brand understands that hair tells the world who we are before we even introduce ourselves to others. The way someone wears their hair can express confidence, creativity, rebellion, tradition, or vulnerability without using any words. Every individual possesses their own dialect, which is a personal way of expressing identity, emotion, and attitude through style. As hairdressers, the team also has a unique dialect formed by techniques, creativity, and professional artistry that shape work. When these dialects meet in the salon, something new is created through a conversation written in cut and colour. This collection represents where personal expression and professional craft collide to form a new visual vocabulary for the future.
The Dialekt Collection serves as a bridge between the vision of the hairdresser and the identity of the client. Under the creative direction of Cos Sakkas, the global artistic team has crafted looks that speak to modern sensibilities. Hair functions as a silent communicator that conveys deep personal messages about a person to the surrounding world today. By focusing on the unique dialect of the wearer, TONI&GUY highlights the diversity found within their global community. Each cut and colour combination is designed to amplify the natural attitude and emotion of the individual model. This approach ensures that the hair remains an authentic representation of the person while showcasing high-level technical skill. The result is a collection that feels both globally relevant and deeply personal to every unique hair texture.
In the Dialekt Collection, the professional craft of the hairdresser is celebrated as a vital form of artistic communication. The global artistic team uses specific techniques to translate abstract emotions into tangible shapes and vibrant hair colour palettes. These methods form the professional dialect that sets TONI&GUY apart as a leading authority in the hair industry. By merging professional expertise with the client’s personal style, a new visual vocabulary is established for the upcoming year. This interaction allows for the creation of styles that are both innovative and wearable for a diverse clientele. The collection emphasizes that hair is not just a trend but a powerful medium for storytelling and self-expression. Through this lens, every haircut becomes a sentence and every colour choice adds a layer of nuanced meaning.
Creative Direction:Cos Sakkas, Global Creative Director.Hair:TONI&GUY Global Artistic Team.Photography:Kevin Luchmun, David Mannah, Ronan Park, Alex Barron-Hough and Chris Bulezuik.Make-up:Kelly Mendiola, Monnie Kaur, Adnan Achirila, Saphron Morgan and Hannah Davies.Styling:Born Apriraskian and Gia.

trend_name: Hair Extension Services: Five Proven Strategies to Attract New Salon Clients
description:
Attracting new interest for hair extension services requires a strategic approach that taps into the seasonal desires of your clientele. Once you introduce someone to the wonders of extensions, they often become one of your most loyal salon clients. Sometimes people just need an added incentive to try hair extension services for the first time in your chair.Hair extensions expertInanch Emirsuggests five ways you can attract new clients throughout the entire calendar year. Promoting the transformative power of these services helps build a consistent revenue stream for your professional hair business. Providing a diverse range of options ensures that every client finds a look that suits their personal lifestyle.
As we head into a new season, many people think about fresh starts and making significant style changes. Devise a hair transformation package to promote hair extension services along with various popular salon add-on services today. Highlight all the benefits of extensions, such as adding length, volume, or a splash of colour without commitment. Pair the service with a free consultation, a conditioning treatment, or a helpful styling tutorial for the client. Offer the exact texture of their natural hair with Gold Glass extensions which include straight, wavy and curly textures. You could further encourage uptake by offering discounts if clients book themselves in before a specific promotional date.
Brides always want an extra-special look on their big day, so do not miss this prime marketing opportunity. Promote your hair extension services as part of a comprehensive wedding hair package for brides and their bridesmaids. You can create jaw-droppingly beautiful wedding looks by adding cascading curls or intricate updos using high-quality hair extensions. When you have the initial consultation with the bride-to-be, discuss all the ways extensions create photo-ready looks. Talk about the various attachment methods and how the look can be adapted after the wedding ceremony is over. This ensures the bride feels confident and beautiful throughout her entire wedding journey and honeymoon period.
Festival season is a prime opportunity to showcase the fun and creative potential of professional hair extension services. You could offer bespoke festival-ready packages ranging from colourful clip-ins to longer, voluminous locks for boho-chic styles. This is a great way to introduce hair extension services to a younger demographic in your local area. Consider partnering with local influencers or hosting a festival hair prep day at your salon for new clients. Highlighting the ease of removal and maintenance makes these services perfect for festival goers wanting a bold look. These temporary options allow clients to experiment with their style without making a long-term commitment to one look.
Hair goes through a lot during summer and by autumn it often needs some serious professional care and attention. While recommending nourishing moisturising treatments to rehydrate damaged hair is a must, also promote your hair extension services. Extensions offer a great way to give clients a post-holiday confidence boost while their natural hair recovers. A professional service can offer an almost instant fresh look without harming the real hair of the client. This helps banish post-holiday blues and sets your clients up for a stylish and confident autumn season ahead. Promoting these benefits reminds clients that extensions are a versatile tool for hair health and aesthetic beauty.
Despite the fact that the festive season is a distant memory, clients are always looking for head-turning looks for their next big event or party. Promote your hair extension services as the ultimate accessory. Create glamorous party packages such as Red-Carpet Glamour or Party Queen Glitz to entice your existing salon followers. Promote these various themes on your website to capture people searching for holiday hair inspiration in your city. You could also bundle extensions in with a pre-party blow-dry service to ensure the look wows everyone. These targeted promotions help fill your column during the busiest time of the year for the industry.
For more information, visitinanch.com
